title: Rottlera inophyllum
intro: Rottlera inophyllum, also known as amla tree or kathale or mullakathala, is a deciduous tree native to tropical Asia, valued for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Rottlera inophyllum include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-diabetic properties, which are thought to aid in the prevention and treatment of various diseases. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. Rottlera inophyllum is used to make various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are commonly used to treat fever, respiratory infections, and other health conditions.
taxonomy: Rottlera inophyllum is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It is a tropical tree native to India and Southeast Asia. The plant is classified in the genus Rottlera. Rottlera inophyllum is a deciduous tree that grows up to 15 meters tall. It is also known as Kambo or Kambo tree.
uses: Rottlera inophyllum has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It has been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic properties. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions. Additionally, Rottlera inophyllum has been used to manage respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these uses, but it has shown promise in traditional medicine.
benefits: Rottlera inophyllum, also known as the "Indian liver-plant," has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of various diseases. Rottlera inophyllum is also used to treat digestive issues and is thought to have a laxative effect, helping to regulate bowel movements. In addition, the plant is said to have anti-microbial properties, which may aid in the treatment of infections and wounds. Further studies are needed to confirm the efficacy of Rottlera inophyllum's health benefits.
properties: Rottlera inophyllum is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine and has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties. The leaves and bark of the plant are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Rottlera inophyllum has been reported to exhibit immunomodulatory effects, making it a potential candidate for the treatment of various diseases. Additionally, the plant has been studied for its antidiabetic, antihypertensive, and anti-cancer properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Rottlera inophyllum.
constituents: Rottlera inophyllum contains a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. These active constituents are responsible for its medicinal properties and have been studied for their potential health benefits. Flavonoids, in particular, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Alkaloids, such as rotlinine, have been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties, while saponins have been reported to have antidiarrheal effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the active constituents and their effects on human health.
parts: Rottlera inophyllum is known for its medicinal properties, with the leaves being a key component. The leaves of this plant are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's roots, bark, and seeds are also used in traditional medicine in some regions. The roots and bark are said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, while the seeds are used to treat fever and other ailments. The plant's medicinal parts are also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine systems.
preparations: Rottlera inophyllum is a plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurvedic practices. Herbal preparations made from its leaves are believed to have various health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves are often used to treat fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions. The plant is also said to have antimicrobial properties, which may help in wound healing. side_effects: Rottlera inophyllum can cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its alkaloid content. The plant may also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. Some individuals may experience allergic reactions, including skin rashes and itching, after exposure to Rottlera inophyllum. In high doses, the plant may cause cardiac problems, including arrhythmias and decreased blood pressure. Pregnant women should avoid using Rottlera inophyllum due to potential risks to the fetus.
